title: Scale test: Validate gateway-centric mode
description:
Validate that gateway-centric mode reduces channel overhead at scale.

## Test scenarios
1. 100 nodes with gateway - measure announce rate before/after DODAG join
2. 300 nodes with gateway - verify ~4% overhead vs ~24% without mode
3. Gateway failure - verify nodes resume normal announces within 60 sec
4. Mixed: some nodes in DODAG, some orphaned - verify correct behavior

## Metrics
- Announce rate per node (should drop 6x when DODAG joined)
- Total channel utilization (target: <5% overhead with gateway)
- Recovery time on DODAG loss

## Success criteria
- Channel overhead with gateway-centric mode < 5%
- Normal announces resume within 60 sec of DODAG loss
- No peer-to-peer regression (LOADng still works)
close_reason: Gateway-centric mode implemented across simulator, Rust, C/Zephyr, scale test: DIO flag parsed and propagated to DodagState, announce scheduler uses dynamic interval (300s normal, 1800s gateway) with reschedule on state change and 60s grace on DODAG loss, LOADng fallback enforced in routing decision when gradients stale, all tests pass including interop and scale (4% overhead), spec conformance verified.
